    "R1.1": "The section asserts a specific gap (expensive ESM compute), a mechanism (confidence-gated routing), and a contribution scope (cost-effective forecasting for municipal planners); validating whether the framing is coherent and accurate is a standard review expectation.",
    "R1.2": "Multiple internal logical tensions are present: a 6.2% compute reduction is characterized as a 'substantial advance' and 'fundamental shift'; the only comparison baseline is uniform random subsampling, which is too weak to support a SOTA claim; the section simultaneously treats CMIP5 as the gold standard while acknowledging CMIP6 is newer. These require load-bearing consistency evaluation.",
    "R1.3": "The section's strongest claims ('new standard,' 'fundamental shift,' 'broad deployment') are disproportionate to the reported evidence: one metric (6.2% reduction), one accuracy figure (0.08°C MAD), and a single weak baseline. Verifying that claims are grounded in what is actually demonstrated is load-bearing here.",
    "R1.4": "Novelty is asserted against only a uniform-sampling baseline, with no engagement with existing learned-routing or surrogate-model literature. Assessing whether the delta vs. prior work justifies SOTA positioning is centrally expected.",
    "R1.7": "Using CMIP5 (2012) as the gold-standard reference while CMIP6 (the successor ensemble, released ~2019) is demoted to an appendix sanity check raises a significant scholarship concern; the choice of reference ensemble directly affects all reported error figures and SOTA claims."

rationale.R1.1: The section asserts a specific gap (expensive ESM compute), a mechanism (confidence-gated routing), and a contribution scope (cost-effective forecasting for municipal planners); validating whether the framing is coherent and accurate is a standard review expectation.
rationale.R1.2: Multiple internal logical tensions are present: a 6.2% compute reduction is characterized as a 'substantial advance' and 'fundamental shift'; the only comparison baseline is uniform random subsampling, which is too weak to support a SOTA claim; the section simultaneously treats CMIP5 as the gold standard while acknowledging CMIP6 is newer. These require load-bearing consistency evaluation.
rationale.R1.3: The section's strongest claims ('new standard,' 'fundamental shift,' 'broad deployment') are disproportionate to the reported evidence: one metric (6.2% reduction), one accuracy figure (0.08°C MAD), and a single weak baseline. Verifying that claims are grounded in what is actually demonstrated is load-bearing here.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty is asserted against only a uniform-sampling baseline, with no engagement with existing learned-routing or surrogate-model literature. Assessing whether the delta vs. prior work justifies SOTA positioning is centrally expected.
rationale.R1.7: Using CMIP5 (2012) as the gold-standard reference while CMIP6 (the successor ensemble, released ~2019) is demoted to an appendix sanity check raises a significant scholarship concern; the choice of reference ensemble directly affects all reported error figures and SOTA claims.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review assess the technical and scientific content — logic, evidence-claim alignment, scholarship, and novelty — or is it a prose and writing-quality pass only?
